title: Only A Sod
content:
آ آ آ آ It's only a sod, but ï؟½twill break me ould heart
آ آ آ آ Nigh hardened wid toilinï؟½ and carinï؟½,
آ آ آ آ And make the ould wounds in it tingle and smart.
آ آ آ آ Itï؟½s only a sod, but itï؟½s parcel and part
آ آ آ آ Of strugglinï؟½, sufferinï؟½ Erin.

آ آ آ آ Itï؟½s only a sod, but it rakes the ould pain,
آ آ آ آ The ould love in me heart that still lingers,
آ آ آ آ That Time has been soothing and docthï؟½ring in vain;
آ آ آ آ And now he must soothe it and heal it again
آ آ آ آ Wid his kindly and gentle ould fingers.

آ آ آ آ Itï؟½s only a sod, but I see a big ship
آ آ آ آ Through the gallopinï؟½ waters come tearinï؟½,
آ آ آ آ And a lass that looks back on the horizon dip,
آ آ آ آ Wid eyes full of tears and a thrimblinï؟½ lip,
آ آ آ آ On the last that she saw of ould Erin.

آ آ آ آ Itï؟½s only a sod, but wid care it will keep
آ آ آ آ Till me brooms and me brushes are silint
آ آ آ آ Put it into me arms ere they bury me deep,
آ آ آ آ And tell them old Biddy the ï؟½slaveyï؟½ does sleep
آ آ آ آ ï؟½Neath a sod from the bogs of ould Irlint.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Henry Lawson represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance, inviting the reader to reflect on the deeper themes of human experience and artistic expression.

In the broader context of Henry Lawson's bibliography, these specific lines stand out for their clarity and poignancy. Whether you are searching for inspiration or a moment of quiet contemplation, this work offers a timeless perspective that transcends its original era.

At Linespedia, we have curated this specific poem to ensure it reaches a modern audience. We recommend sharing these lines as a digital poster or using them as a thoughtful caption to connect with others who appreciate the enduring beauty of verse.
